meaning: n.衣服,浅黑浆糊vt.痛殴,击倒,使惨败
examples: The police intended to clobber drunk drivers.
Babies and small children require an inordinate amount of clobber.
If you want to "clobber" existing files as the rename progresses, add the -f flag.
At some point, of course, high prices will clobber demand and encourage efficiency, fuel switching and so on.
Tax was a key theme of the election and one with which the Tories hoped they could clobber Labour.
